segment-text: Tony Lord & David Cohen
April 20th, 2009 INT. HUTCHINSON HOME - BATHROOM - MORNING
HARVEY HUTCHINSON, 44, confident, full of ambition, and in his underwear, grooms himself for work while listening to a motivational PODCAST through headphones.
You want to reach the top of the ladder, my friend? Then grab your balls and take no prisoners. It's kill or be killed...
Harvey poses in the mirror like a gunslinger. He quick draws one hand like a pistol, grabs his balls with the other, then pulls the imaginary trigger. Bam!
INT. HUTCHINSON HOME - MORNING
Harvey's dressed for success and lookin' sharp. He approaches his SON'S BEDROOM, sniffs the air and stops. He holds his nose to block the smell and enters without a knock.
INT. HUTCH'S BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS

A disaster area. Dirty clothes and fast food wrappers litter the floor. FHM girls and NASCAR posters cover the walls. Harvey spots the source of the stink in the corner. A MOLDY, BUG INFESTED, BURRITO SUPREME.
Hutch! I told you to clean this
room and not to leave foodHe YANKS back the blanket. But Hutch's bed is empty. Harvey looks over to see the family dog, RACHAEL RAY, a portly pug, scarfing down the moldy burrito remains.
Rachael Ray, NO! That's disgusting.
INT. HUTCHINSON HOME - LIVING ROOM - MORNING
Harvey comes down the stairs. His blossoming daughter, NICOLE HUTCHINSON, 14, sits on the couch, working on her laptop.
Morning, Nic. Hey.
He notices her t-shirt: A billiard rack with "NICE" in the middle.
You leave the house in that shirt, you're grounded for life.
Why? It just means I'm good at shooting pool.
He gives her a knowing look.
Are you going to help me with my video project for school like you keep promising?
You need to get the VHS tapes transferred to your computer like I told you.
She points to her laptop.
Done. A week ago. Like I told you yesterday.
Good. We'll work on it Saturday.
Yeah, right. And I'm banging the Jonas Brothers.
Watch your mouth.
From the kitchen comes MARILYN HUTCHINSON, 42, attentive mom and real estate agent. She hands Harvey a cup of coffee.
Breakfast?
Running late. Where's Hutch? Sleeping?
He's not in his room. And I've told him a dozen times not to leave food in there, it smells like Tijuana.
Nicole comments on the transferred video on her computer.
Nice hair in the 80's, Mom. You
look John Mayer.
Everyone's hair was bad in the 80's, Nic. (to Harvey) Dinner?
Working late. Don't wait.
Remember our talk about balance?
That's why I have you.
He gives her a quick kiss and is out the door.
EXT. HUTCHINSON HOME - BACKYARD - MORNING

Harvey spots a MILLER BEER CAN on the lawn...and then another, and another. The trail of cans lead to the POOL where Harvey sees... HUTCH HUTCHINSON, 21, a soon-to-be college junior disguised as a shaggy mess of a slacker, is PASSED OUT ON A RAFT in his clothes from the night before. Harvey grabs the pool skimmer and FLIPS THE RAFT, DUMPING HUTCH INTO THE WATER.
WHAT THE HELL?
Good morning, sunshine.
That was not cool, Dad.
Neither are the maggots living in your room.
He tosses an empty Miller can at Hutch.
Or you bringing the competition into my home.
They were out of Hibrau.
You shouldn't be drinking anyway. I'm 21.
Then start acting like it.
Harvey glances over at his prized ROSE BUSHES.
My babies look dry. Tell me you're sticking to the watering schedule?
C'mon. Can you stop harassing me about everything?
I'm the boss. Someday you'll have a kid then you'll be the boss. Until then I need you to pick up lawn bags and a new rake at the hardware store.
I don't have any cash. Shocker.
Harvey takes out his wallet and hands Hutch his Am-Ex card.
Let's get on it. And don't use the card for anything else, got me?
Harvey gives him a condescending grin, then heads off.
INT./EXT. HIBRAU BREWING CO. - ESTABLISHING SHOTS

The hustle and bustle of a mid-size brewery in action. FACTORY WORKERS and EXECS showing up to work. - BREWMASTERS tasting their mixture. - Bottles filled and capped on the assembly line. - A FORKLIFT DRIVER hauls cases of Hibrau.
EXT. HIBRAU BREWING CO. - PARKING LOT - MORNING

Harvey parks his Lincoln Navigator in the huge lot. As he walks through the rows, he stops to admire the high-end European sedans in the "RESERVED EXECUTIVE PARKING" section. Near the main building, Harvey stops at a statue of OLDJONES, a guy from the 1880's. He's sporting a top hat and tails and holding up a bottle of Hibrau. A plaque reads: "HIBRAU LAGER - MILWAUKEE'S LOYAL FRIEND SINCE 1883".
(grabbing his balls) You and me, Jonesy. We take no prisoners.
INT. HIBRAU BREWING CO. - MARKETING DEPT. - MORNING
Harvey zigzags through a maze of cubicles. Promotional displays and test market products are scattered everywhere. Outside his modest office, he's greeted by his assistant, JANIS, 50.
Morning, Mr. Hutchinson.
Good morning, Janis. Third quarter sales report?
She hands it to him. He flips to the last page.
Ouch. Shit is going to hit the fan. (beat) (MORE)
But like The Donald says, "Sometimes by losing a battle you find a new way to win the war."
Staff meeting's in ten minutes.
Bring it on.
He tucks the sales report under his arm and heads off.
INT. HIBRAU BREWING CO. - CONFERENCE ROOM - DAY
CEO, ARTHUR BRACKEN, 64, a tough as nails silver fox, paces around the conference table that includes Harvey and the rest of the marketing department. They are listening to Senior
V.P. of Sales, ELMER WINTHORPE, 60.
Winthorpe points to a MOCK-UP AD of Old-Jones, wearing his top-hat, in front of a fire with a cat curled up on his lap.
And then Old-Jones says, "What do you think, Pussy? Shall we crack open another Hibrau? And then the cat says, "One loyal friend deserves another."
Bracken rubs his temples. Frustrated.
The cat talks?
He says, "One loyal friend deserves another."
Has Old-Jones gone senile? Is that why he talks to a cat?
I'm sorry, Arthur?
Old-Jones, the iconic symbol of the Hibrau Brewing company, is having a conversation with a cat, so I'm
assuming he's developed
Um, no, the uh...
Shut up, Winthorpe! And we're wondering why sales are off twenty percent? Does anyone here have any good ideas, for God's sake? (beat) HELLO? SOMEONE? ANYONE?
Harvey confidently stands up.
The cat meows. Bracken stares at Harvey for a long beat.
Pussy knows what it wants, Arthur.
I like it! Good work Hutchinson. Make it happen.
INT. HIBRAU BREWING CO. - CONFERENCE ROOM - LATER
As the meeting breaks up, Harvey files out. His go-with-theflow buddy, PHIL DAVIS, 40's, comes over.
Gutsy move, Harv.
You want to get to the top? Sometimes you grab your nut sack.
Interesting concept.
KEITH PITTMAN, 38, your typical back stabbing co-worker, slaps Harvey on the shoulder.
Smooth move, Hutchinson. Better grease up the next kayak to Kenosha.
Pittman, if you're going to make an insult, it helps if people have a clue as to what you're talking about.
Well just remember what happened to the last guy that a took steamer on one of Winthorpe's ideas.
You think I'm worried aboutWinthorpe beelines over. He's not happy.
MY OFFICE, ONE HOUR. Yes, sir.
Pittman smirks as Harvey fights to keep his composure.
INT. HUTCHINSON HOME - HUTCH'S ROOM - DAY

Rachael Ray is perched on Hutch's lap as he and his buddies down cans of BUDWEISER and play NASCAR '09 on PlayStation. There's Hutch's better half and BFF, TEDDY O'Neil, 21. The book smart, 5 foot 3, RILEY FORD, 21. And GREG WARBURTON, 22, he can rebuild an engine and kick your ass at the same time.
See that, Ted-O? That was Jeff Gordon dusting your redneck Dale Earnhardt ass. Riley, hold on to your scrote-bag 'cause here I come.
Hutch's fingers dance expertly over his controller as his Jeff Gordon car motors past Riley's Jimmi Johnson car.
Sick move, Hutch. Guess there is an upside to you playing this thing twelve hours a day.
Jealousy is a very ugly quality, Warburton. But I will admit, if I could major in NASCAR '09, I'd contemplate going back to school.
You tell Harv yet that you're taking the year off?
Waiting for the right moment.
He's going to flip his shit.
Might. But I've got my life to think about, right?
(quick taps controller) Laters, Warburton.
Hutch leaves Warburton's Kyle Bush in his wake and TAKES THE CHECKERED FLAG. Rachael Ray BARKS her congrats.
Chicken dinner, ladies!
Riley tosses a victory Budweiser to Hutch. He toasts virtual Jeff Gordon on the TV.
Hells bells, Wonder Boy.
He swigs the beer then gives Rachael Ray her share.
That was the last one. Beer run?
INT. WINTHORPE'S OFFICE - DAY
Harvey sits across from Winthorpe in his plush corner office.
Hutchinson, who plucked you from the warehouse at twenty-five?
You did, Elmer.
And who gave you your first promotion, your last promotion and every damn promotion in between?
You, Elmer.
AND YOU STAB ME IN THE BACK!
I was just-
Do you know what Bracken said to me after the meeting? That my ideas were antiquated.
Winthorpe points to a decades old logo of Old-Jones.
I created Old-Jones in '72...
Winthorpe SHOVES a recent bottle of Hibrau in Harvey's face. Same exact logo.
And the top-hatted son of a bitch is still selling beer today! That's not a coincidence, that's staying power, Hutchinson. Something you clearly don't have anymore! Elmer-
WE'RE DONE. GET OUT OF MY FACE!
Fuming pissed, Winthorpe spins his chair, turning his back. Harvey gets up and shuffles out. Agggh!
Harvey turns back.
No answer. Harvey closes the door behind him.
INT. WARBURTON'S JEEP WRANGLER - DAY
Hutch and crew cruise in Warburton's ridiculously jacked up Bronco.
You really should consider joining me, Ted-O. A year off from school could be the experience of a lifetime. No classes, sleeping 'till noon, then getting lit the rest of the day.
Uh, we've been doing that all summer.
Yeah, but how cool would it be to do it in colder weather? Just tell your pops you need to "find yourself" or something.
He's right, Teddy. You're wasting your time with that shit.
What, getting an education so he
doesn't end up at Auto Zone?
Funny one, little man. Least I make bank. Lot of good that diploma's done you.
I'm still weighing my employment opportunities.
Bet they weigh less then you.
Warburton, 7-11 on your left.
INT. 7-11 - DAY
Hutch and Teddy hit the beer fridge. Hutch scans right past the Hibrau and grabs a 12 pack of COORS LIGHT.
I'm feeling like the cold refreshment of the Rockies. Cool?
Lock and load the Silver Bullets, baby. Grab two.
He dumps two 12's in Teddy's arms.
I'm cashed out and you're up.
Teddy searches his pockets. He pulls out a crumpled coupon.
All I got is a 1/2 off on a Domino's two topping.
Hutch contemplates. Then pulls out Harvey's gold Am-EX.
The things I sacrifice for us. Meet you outside.
INT. 7-11 - COUNTER - MOMENTS LATER
A kind-of-cute CASHIER, 21, rings up the beer.
Hey, I remember you from High School. Hutch Hutchinson, right?
In the flesh.
Hutch hands over Harvey's credit card.
Wow, Gold Card. You must be doing well?
Some of us are just destined for greatness.
She looks at the name on card.
Yeah, right. Still using Daddy's card are we?
Uh, Hutch is short for Harvey...in Dubai. I'm half Dubai...a-nin. Nice try.
Hutch GRABS an assortment of crappy counter items including lighters, key chains, calling cards and chick magazines.
Would I buy all these personal gifts for you, including fifty of those Lucky 7 lotto scratchers, if this wasn't my card?
Seriously? You're going to buy me all this stuff?
If you give me your phone number, foxy lady, this is all yours.
You're cute. Make it a hundred lotto scratchers and you got a deal. Done.
EXT. HIBRAU BREWING CO. - DAY
Out by the Old-Jones statue, Harvey vents to Phil.
He's not going to fire you, Harv. You know how Winthorpe gets.
He was practically foaming at the mouth. Ungrateful S.O.B. is lucky I saved his ass in that meeting.
Harvey looks up at Old-Jones. Has a moment.
You know what? I'm done being bullied. I'm marching into Winthorpe's office, tell him he can
go to hell andHarvey and Phil JUMP BACK as an AMBULANCE screeches to a halt right in front of them. PARAMEDICS rush out and thunder into
the building. INT. HIBRAU BREWING CO. - OFFICES - DAY
The elevator doors open. Harvey and Phil step out to see the Paramedics at work as employees crowd around WINTHORPE'S OFFICE. Harvey moves in closer. Oh my God!
The Paramedics lift a rigor stiff WINTHORPE off his chair and place him on a the gurney. Pittman steps up next to Harvey.
Looks like Elmer bought the farm...but you ran away with all the pigs. What?
SARGENT HEINRICK, a Nazi-like Hibrau security guard approaches Harvey.
Mr. Hutchinson, it appears that you were the last one to see Mr. Winthorpe alive. His last words to you were probably his last words on earth.
As they wheel Winthorpe past Harvey, he observes the FROZEN ANGRY GRIMACE on Winthorpe's face.
He said, uh, "Good job". Sargent Heinrick makes some notes and follows the paramedics. Mr. Bracken comes over to Harvey.
Elmer Winthorpe, he was a good man, Arthur. This is a real tragedy.
(leans in, whispers) It's a good thing he bit the dust, otherwise he'd be out of a job.
Harvey shivers at Bracken's coldness.
I'm not saying anything, Hutchinson, but Elmer's shoes need to be filled and I've got an idea of who's going to be doing the filling.
Harvey smiles, quickly getting over the loss.
INT. HUTCHINSON HOME - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
Harvey rants like a hyper puppy as the family eats dinner. Hutch sneaks Rachael Ray spaghetti under the table.
Of course I'm upset that Winthorpe's dead. But it was his time. And now it's mine.
You're a monster, Harv. Agreed.
You guys won't be saying that after I become a Vice President. It's going to change everything.
That's what that Cheney dude said.
Hutch drops Rachael Ray a handful of pasta. She INHALES it.
Hutch, are you feeding the dog from the table? What? No.
We'll be able to get into the Fair Oaks Country Club. Go on great vacations with the other execs.
I'd rather become a hooker.
Harvey's on a roll. Doesn't even hear her.
Nicole, enough.
And guess who'll be watching Bucks games from the company floor seats?
Jack Nicholson?
Bracken already bumped me to the top of the list for tickets.
Hutch slyly palms a huge meatball, tosses it to Rachael Ray.
You haven't even been offered the job, Harvey.
Arthur practically guaranteed it. We just need a little time. Out of respect for Winthorpe.
Aren't you the saint.
Rachael Ray RIPS a gas bomb. Harvey glares at Hutch.
I'm not feeding her!
I've waited a long time for this promotion and fate has finally arrived.
Harvey's feeling good. Hutch takes the opportunity.
Speaking of fate, I, uh, have a small announcement.
We already know, you're gay.
Funny one. (beat) So, I've decided to take things in a new direction with school.
Harvey sports a big grin.
You've decided on a major! That's great, Hutch. Business or marketing?
Well, neither. (beat) I'm, uh, taking a year off from school.
Ouch. Didn't see that one coming.
Before you go psycho, just listen. This plan helps out all of us.
Marilyn puts her hand on Harvey's to calm him down. Go ahead.

Okay. Since I have yet to clearly define my career path, it makes smart business sense, especially in these tough economic times, to evaluate the various opportunities out there. So why not take a year off, really research this thing, and "find myself"? Then bang! I'm back in school, heading toward a career and a life filled with huge financial rewards and tons of emotional stability. To top things off, you guys won't have to worry about cash if things get really bad. 'Ol Hutch here will unselfishly be able to provide for the whole family and we'll live happily ever after. (like Barack Obama) Yes, we can.
Silence. They all stare at him. He smiles back.
Call me when the shit storm blows over.
Nicole gets up and leaves.
That's it? That's your plan?
Well I might travel a little in between the research. You know what they say, "All work and no play"HARVEY ARE YOU INSANE? Do you know how hard I work so you can go to college? A luxury I never had!
Hutch, this might not be the right decision.
I'm just not sure if school's my thing right now.
I'll tell you one thing, mister, it's going to look really bad that a Vice President's son is a college drop out!
Hutch is put off by Harvey's comment.
Harvey, calm down. That's not the issue here.
I've made up my mind. I'm not going back.
Okay smart guy, what are you going to do for money? Because you're not free loading around this house like you've done all summer.
I already told you, I'll be doing research.
If you're not going to school, then you're getting a job! Then you'll see how lucky you are to be in college. Tomorrow morning you are out pounding the pavement. No more free rides here, pal.
Harvey storms away from the table, but STOPS as Rachael Ray blocks his path, staring up at him.
Go on! Move it, girl.
Rachael Ray convulses...then PUKES SPAGHETTI on Harvey's
HUUUUTCH!
INT. HUTCHINSON HOME - HUTCH'S ROOM - MORNING
Harvey enters and BANGS on his briefcase like a drum.
OKAY, MR. FIND YOURSELF GUY, TIME
But Hutch isn't there. On top of it, the bed is made. Harvey shrugs. Maybe he's getting through to him.
INT. HARVEY'S CAR - MORNING
Harvey drives to work, listening to more motivational crap.
Now say it! Nothing will stand in my way of success. I will get what I want.
Nothing will stand in my way of success! I will get what I want.
SUDDENLY, in the rearview mirror, A HALF NAKED GIRL pops up from the back seat. It's the CASHIER from 7-11.
Where am I?
WWWAAAAAAAAA!
Harvey SWERVES out of traffic and pulls the car to the curb.
WHO ARE YOU?
The last thing I remember is we were smoking this killer weed. WHO'S WE?
Harvey sees HUTCH PASSED OUT in the back in a sea of junk food wrappers and slurpee cups. Harvey smacks him awake. Hutch looks up. Shit.
EXT. 7-11 - LATER
Cashier hurries out of the car, fumbling with her clothes. Hutch leans out the window.
I'll hit you later, cool?
Screw you, loser. I knew you were full of crap!
Harvey looks at Hutch and shakes his head.
Look Dad, IHARVEY Not a word. Not one word!
INT. HIBRAU BREWING CO. - BREWERY - DAY
Harvey drags Hutch over to OSCAR, 50's, brewery foreman.
Hey Oscar, How's it goin'? Harvey!
You remember my son, Hutch?
Sure. Sure. You were bussing tables at your dad's BBQ last summer.
That's me. Jack of all trades...and slave labor.
Tell me Harv, how are things in white-collar world?
I'm still the same guy, just wearing a different uniform.
Hutch rolls his eyes.
Speaking of uniforms, baseball starts in a week. This is our year.

Gonna crush those wimps from Pabst! So Oscar, Hutch here is looking for a job and I thought this would be a great place for him to start. He'll work hard and you can pay him minimum wage. What do you say?
Of course. If he's anything like his old man he'll be running the place in no time.
I'm nothing like him.
I always tell the boys in the warehouse, if you think this is the end of the road, just look at Harvey Hutchinson. He's proof that there's no limit to your dreams.
You speak the truth, my friend. (walking off) Put the kid to work.
INT. HIBRAU BREWING CO. - EXECUTIVE FLOOR - DAY
Harvey passes Winthorpe's old corner office. He peeks INSIDE to see painters and carpet guys changing it up.
(like a mad scientist) Soon you will be mine, all mine.
INT. HIBRAU BREWING CO. - BREWERY - DAY
Hutch is drenched with sweat and miserable as he lifts cases of beer off the assembly line and onto a pallet. Musclehead co-worker, Jake, 30's, works effortlessly alongside Hutch.
Told ya this is an awesome workout, Hutchinson. FEEL THE BURN!
Hutch can hardly breath. He stops and leans over.
Uh, huh. I need a sec.
Well, hurry up. I gotta get to a veterinarian.
A veterinarian?
Jake flexes his arms.
Yeah, my pythons are sick!
Jake sticks his bicep in Hutch's face.
Feel these bastards!
(creeped out) Seriously, I'm good.
See how thick the vein is? That's from hauling kegs, amigo. Go on, stroke that puppy.
Jake doesn't move his arm. Hutch awkwardly touches it.
I'm not real comfortable with this. VOICE (O.S.) Yo Jake, that your new girlfriend?
They turn around to see, ANGELA, 20's, smokin' hot blue collar chick, sitting in the driver's seat of a forklift. Jake quickly pushes Hutch away. Turns psycho.
GET THE HELL AWAY FROM ME BEFORE I MONKEY HUMP YOUR SKULL!
(terrified) I wasn't...he told me to...
YOUR ELBOWS! Hutch is about to piss his pants as Jake stares him down like a raging bull.
Give the kid a break, Jake. I don't thinks he's your type.
I AM NOT INTO DUDES!
Come on, we're getting backed up.
Angela forks the pallet and steers toward the docks. Jake eases up, puts his hand on Hutch's shoulder.
Sorry about that, Hutchinson. Didn't want her to think I was into weird shit. Thanks for covering.
(weakly) No problem. You're hurting my shoulder.
Jake lets go. Hutch strains to get a final peek at Angela.
I gotta stop mixing the roids with the Red Bull. Gets me a tad zippy.
Never would have known.
OKAY, YOU READY FOR A TRICEP PARTY?
He throws Hutch a stack of cases. Hutch drops to his knees.
HELL YA, BABY! WOOO!
EXT. BASEBALL FIELD - DUGOUT - EVENING
Practice has finished. Harvey and Phil take off their cleats.
(like an announcer) Batting third and playing short stop, Vice President of Sales, Haaaarveeey Huuuthcinson.
He makes crowd cheering noises.
Promotion's looking good, huh?
I'd say any day now.
Harvey pulls a MERCEDES BENZ BROCHURE out of his gym bag.
Check this out. The Mercedes S-600. This baby screams success.
Even nicer with 20" rims and a gun metal metallic paint job. That's how a V.P. rolls, my friend.
INT. HUTCHINSON HOME - BACKYARD - DUSK
Hutch is sprawled out on a lounge chair, exhausted from work. Harvey comes through the gate carrying a garden hose.
Hey pal, how's it goin' in the real world? Are you finding yourself?
(groans) I can't feel my arms.
Not too late to register for classes.
Forget it. You'll never break me.
I like your energy. Use it to clean
out the garage this weekend.
He tosses the hose on top of Hutch.
Roses look hungry. Make them happy. Hutch grumbles as Harvey walks off with a whistle.
INT. HUTCHINSON HOME - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
Nicole's working on her video project. Her laptop is hooked up to the TV which shows A YOUNGER HARVEY DANCING UP A STORM AT A PARTY, circa 1983. Harvey enters with his head in the Mercedes brochure.
Dad, are you gonna help me with this or what?
What's that?
He's completely focused on the brochure.
Taking me to get a boob job.
A little later, honey.
You're not even listening! I need help with my project.
Harvey looks up from the brochure. See's himself on the TV.
Hey, I remember that!
He does a Michael Jackson move from Thriller. Not bad.
Your old man could tear up the dance floor!
Oh-my-God. Don't ever do that in front of me again.
He goes back to the brochure and heads out.
INT. HUTCHINSON HOME - KITCHEN - NIGHT
Marilyn does the dishes while Harvey places some of his freshly clipped roses in a vase.
Hutch has been at the garage for hours.
Good. The new Benz'll need a clean home.
You're being a little hard on him.
All for his own good, Mare. Eventually he'll realize that his butt should be back in school.
INT. HUTCHINSON HOME - GARAGE - SAME
The place is a mess. Hutch and Teddy sport improvised hockey gear with bike helmets, gardening knee pads and golf clubs. Hutch maneuvers an empty beer can toward Teddy.
Five seconds left in the period.
Hutchinson shoots...He scores! The can RICOCHETS past Teddy and SMASHES through a window.
Shit, my ass. The USA just won the gold medal!
Hutch moves a stack of boxes to hide the broken window then goes to the fridge and grabs a couple cans of Harvey's Hibrau. They crack 'em open and guzzle.
You'd think as your buzz heightens, this Hibrau piss water wouldn't continue to taste like my ass.
Speaking of ass, there's going to be a ton of it next week at Fogelman's barbecue.
Great. I have to work. I'm telling you, Ted-O, this job is ruining my life. Then quit.
Can't. Harv will make me go back to school. (reading Hibrau can) "Milwaukee's loyal friend", my ass! I HATE YOU!
Why don't you just get fired?
Hutch thinks for a moment. Grins.
Show me the money.
INT. HUTCHINSON HOME - GARAGE - LATER
A buzzed Hutch and Teddy are deep into a twelve pack as Hutch pounds away on his laptop.

(Reading as he types) "...and furthermore, Hibrau tastes like watered down near beer and the marketing has no appeal to anyone under fifty. No hot chicks in the ads, sponsorship at sporting events or cool dudes in movies chugging it. Just some lame old top-hat dude on the brink of death. The bottom line is that Hibrau sucks and everything associated with it makes me think of my great grandfather. And he's dead."
That's awesome! It'll get you canned for sure. Maybe even sued.
And here's the thing, I'm just telling the truth. Jerry Maguire's got nothing on me. I'm sending it. Company wide.
On his computer, Hutch selects the e-mail folder, "all@hibrau.com", and hits send.
INT. HARVEY'S OFFICE - MORNING
Janis intercepts Harvey as he heads to his office.
Mr. Hutchinson, Mr. Bracken just called an emergency meeting in the brewery. The entire company.
Meeting? About what?
Might have something to do with your great grandfather. The one who's dead.
What are you talking about?
INT. HIBRAU BREWING CO. - BREWERY - DAY
The place is filling up with execs, office staff and brewery workers. Harvey enters.
Wow. Ballsy move, Harv. Huh?
Another co-worker gives him the thumbs-up.
From the FRONT of the brewery Bracken WAVES Hutch's email.
Hutchinson, come up here now! Harvey points to himself, "Me?"
INT. HIBRAU BREWING CO. - BREWERY - MOMENTS LATER
Up at that front Harvey scans Hutch's email. Shocked.
I had high hopes for you, Hutchinson. I knew you had that something, but thisHARVEY Arthur, I swear, I did not write this email.
I've worked for this company for 25 years. I respect what we represent. I would never say these things.
I'm puzzled, Hutchinson. The email came from H. Hutchinson?
(leans in, whispers) That's my son, Hutch. He's been working in the warehouse. I
Well then, it seems that things have taken an interesting turn.
INT. HIBRAU BREWING CO. - BREWERY - LATER
Bracken reads Hutch's email out loud to the entire company. Harvey stands off to the side, cringing with every word.

"...and the marketing has no appeal to anyone under fifty. No hot chicks in the ads, sponsorship at sporting events or cool dudes in movies chugging it. Just some lame old top-hat dude on the brink of death."
(sotto) I am so screwed.
"The bottom line is that Hibrau sucks and everything associated with it makes me think of my great grandfather. And he's dead." (he scans the crowd) Where is Hutch Hutchinson?
IN THE BACK OF THE ROOM
Hutch hides his grin, ready to face the firing squad and get the hell out of there.
Right here, sir. I'm Hutch Hutchinson.
Come up here, son.
(sotto) It's party time.
The crowd parts as Hutch struts up to Bracken. He passes Harvey who shoots him daggers.
(mouthing) You are dead, mister.
Hutch makes his way next to Bracken.
You wrote this? Yes, sir.
